title: Interaction-Split Edge Spectral Flow and Neutral Triplet Boundary Modes in a C = 2 Hubbard Pump
authors: Yong-Feng Yang, Zhao-Rui Tian, Chen Cheng, Hong-Gang Luo
abstract: We show that a sliding spatial modulation of the on-site Hubbard interaction realizes a many-body Thouless pump whose boundary spectral flow is reconstructed by correlations. For a period-three Hubbard chain at filling $ρ=2/3$, density-matrix renormalization group (DMRG) calculations identify a correlated insulator with many-body Chern number $C=+2$, corresponding to two units of charge pumped per cycle. Its excitation spectrum separates the charge gap from the lowest neutral spin gap, revealing an interacting bulk response beyond a simple spin-degenerate band pump. Under open boundary conditions, this contrast becomes even more pronounced. A spin-degenerate Hartree/Aubry-André-Harper reference pump exhibits simultaneous edge flow in the two spin channels, whereas the full modulated Hubbard model suppresses the boundary doublon channel and splits the spectral flow into two distinct edge events. Between these events, the boundary charge is neutralized while a neutral, spinful triplet-like excitation is localized at the edge. The global $μ-U$ phase diagram reveals filling-dependent topology: the $ρ=2/3$ regime remains in the $C=+2$ sector, while $ρ=4/3$ regime undergoes a spin-gap-closing transition from a $C=-2$ pump to a distinct $C=+1$ topological Mott pump.
summary: This paper shows that in a 1D Hubbard pump, strong interactions reconstruct the edge spectral flow, splitting it into two events and creating a localized neutral triplet mode. It also identifies a filling-dependent topological phase transition in the large-U regime. This discovery highlights how correlations can fundamentally alter topological transport properties beyond the single-particle prediction.
main_problem: Investigating how strong electronic correlations reconstruct the boundary spectral flow and create neutral, spinful edge modes in a many-body Thouless pump.
main_result: The study reveals that interactions split the edge spectral flow into two distinct events and stabilize a neutral, spinful triplet-like boundary mode between them, demonstrating a breakdown of the single-particle mean-field picture.
method: Density-Matrix Renormalization Group (DMRG) calculations using Matrix Product States (MPS) with finite-size scaling analysis.
model_or_system: A 1D spinful Hubbard chain with a period-three spatial modulation of the on-site interaction strength $U_i(\phi)$.
key_observables: Many-body Chern number (C), charge gap ($\Delta_c$), spin gap ($\Delta_s$), neutral excitation gap, local charge and spin density, and integrated boundary charge/magnetization.
important_parameters: Hubbard interaction strength (U), chemical potential ($\mu$), pumping phase ($\phi$), and filling factor ($ho = 2/3, 4/3, 1, 5/3$).
main_assumptions: The study assumes the adiabatic limit for the pumping cycle and relies on the accuracy of DMRG for 1D systems.
figures_summary: Figure 1 introduces the model; Figure 3 shows real-space heatmaps of the spatiotemporal evolution of charge and spin excitations.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the modulated Hubbard pump, compares the full model to a Hartree reference, analyzes the $ho=2/3$ regime for edge splitting and neutral modes, explores the $ho=4/3$ regime for topological phase transitions, and concludes with broader implications for correlated topological transport.
why_interesting: It demonstrates a non-perturbative mechanism where interactions decouple spin and charge sectors at a boundary, providing a blueprint for creating neutral topological quasiparticles in correlated systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Schematic of interaction-reconstructed boundary spectral flow. (a) Spinful Hubbard chain at filling ρ = 2/3 with a period-three sliding onsite interaction [Eq. (1)]. The red profile denotes the modulated doublon cost; advancing ϕ translates the pattern. (b) Spin-degenerate Hartree/AAH reference pump. Independent spin channels exhibit simulta- neous edge spectral flow; red and blue packets denote electron- and hole-like edge excitations, while arrows indicate spin. (c) Full modulated Hubbard pump. The onsite repulsion recon- structs the boundary spectral flow. Between the two sepa- rated edge events, schematically labeled ϕ1 and ϕ2, the inter- mediate edge sector is a localized...

FIG. 2. Interaction-split boundary spectral flow and neutral spin edge sector at ρ = 2/3. (a) Single-particle addition and removal energies, ϵ+ and ϵ−, versus the pumping phase ϕ. (b) Evolution of the spin-flip gap ∆s, the lowest neutral excitation gap ∆E(1) ne , and the fermion charge gap ϵ+ −ϵ−. Parameters are L = 72, period p = 3, and interaction strength U = 2.0. Filled markers with dotted lines denote periodic-boundary- condition results, while open markers with solid lines denote open-boundary-condition results. In (a), the periodic-chain addition/removal spectrum remains gapped throughout the pump cycle, whereas the open-chain addition/removal ener- gies approach zero at two...

FIG. 3. Real-space boundary spin-charge separation and neu- tral triplet modes. Spatiotemporal evolution of the boundary excitations over the pumping cycle ϕ. Real-space heatmaps of the (a) local charge (∆⟨ˆni⟩) and (b) spin (∆⟨ˆSz i ⟩) excitations across the 1D lattice. To strictly isolate the topological edge modes, observables are calculated as the difference between the macroscopic Sz tot = 1 spin-flip excitation and the singlet ground state. (c) Integrated charge accumulation ∆QR and magnetization ∆M z R strictly at the right boundary. The tem- poral alignment of the panels reveals the dynamic spatial de- coupling: between the two edge events, the boundary charge is temporarily...

FIG. 4. Ground-state phase diagram and filling-dependent topological pumps. (a) Global µ-U phase diagram of Eq. (1), showing gapless Luttinger-liquid (LL), correlated-insulating (CI), and Mott-insulating (MI) regimes. Numbers inside insu- lating lobes denote the many-body Chern number C. (b),(c) Extrapolated bulk gaps versus U at (b) ρ = 2/3 and (c) ρ = 4/3, obtained under periodic boundary conditions [32]. The plotted quantities are the charge gap ∆c, spin gap ∆s, lowest neutral gap ∆E(1) ne , and pair-binding energy Eb. The top strips indicate the many-body Chern number.
